title: To investorer: Her er den AI-aktie, vi vil købe lige nu
summary_md:
# To investorer: Her er den AI-aktie, vi vil købe lige nu

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-06-24  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Two Danish investors and a financial journalist debate how to invest in AI today, balancing high-growth chip and memory names (Micron, Nvidia, ASML, TSMC) against the market's nervousness and extreme volatility after sharp single-day drops. They examine three Danish C25 stocks with the highest expected 2035 earnings growth — DSV (logistics, acquisition machine), Ambu (endoscopes, a 'show me' turnaround) and Demant (hearing aids) — and discuss AI-resistant physical-product businesses (Hasbro, Rentokil, insurance, pharma) as defensive ballast. The mood is constructively bullish on AI over the long run but cautious near-term, stressing diversification and only sizing AI as part of a portfolio.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 DSV — DSV | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 75%

Best-in-class Nordic acquirer; DB Schenker synergies underestimated by market; EPS seen rising from ~63 to ~223 kr by 2035.

#### Reasoning

Victor argues Nordea's analyst sees a Blue Sky scenario of ~16bn synergies plus cost cuts and price hikes, putting 2028 estimates ~30% above market. With only ~6% global share there is ample room to keep acquiring and compounding. Main risk is failing to deliver synergies and AI/Amazon disruption of logistics.

#### Sell Triggers

- if DB Schenker synergies are not realized by 2028
- if AI logistics models (e.g. new entrants) materially disrupt freight pricing
- if Amazon's internal B2B logistics takes meaningful share

> *"Så vår systemat för 2028 är egentligen att de, om de höstar synergierna som vi förväntar, att de kan havna omkring 30% över marknaden."*

---

### 🟢 NVDA — Nvidia | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 80%

Victor's single top pick; trades ~20x earnings, cheap given growth, strong AI moat and fewer idiosyncratic risks than Alphabet.

#### Reasoning

Asked to pick one stock, Victor chooses Nvidia: it has grown enormously yet trades around 20x, which he calls extremely cheap relative to peers, and lacks Alphabet's search-disruption risk. Part of a strategy of owning moaty AI supply-chain winners rather than speculative names. Risk is a broad AI theme de-rating.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI capex from hyperscalers slows
- if the broad AI theme de-rates ("dot-com" scenario)
- if competition erodes GPU pricing power

> *"Jag vill välja Nvidia. ... Och Nvidia handlar om start 20. Det är bara otroligt billigt i förhållande till så mycket annat."*

---

### 🟢 GOOGL — Alphabet | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Nina's pick as a one-stop AI 'ETF': owns LLM, cloud and can deploy AI internally; recently dipped, offers dollar exposure.

#### Reasoning

Nina would hold/add Alphabet because it has the full AI stack and recently fell, making it a convenient diversified bet. Victor also likes it but flags search (~80% of earnings) facing the SEO→GEO transition where ad monetization is unproven. Held in the Allstar portfolio (though Simon proposes trimming).

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI/generative search erodes core search ad revenue
- if cloud growth disappoints versus AWS/Azure
- if monetization of generative search ('GEO') fails to materialize

> *"Ja, jag vill hålla fast i Alphabet."*

---

### 🟢 FLS — FLSmidth | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Simplified operations lifting profitability; order growth expected to pick up H2 with high metal prices, spare factory capacity = strong incremental earnings.

#### Reasoning

Victor took it into the Danish portfolio: profitability has improved markedly and the missing piece, order intake, should pick up in H2 as high metal prices drive downstream processing demand. With underutilized factories, incremental orders carry little extra cost, so earnings growth could be strong. Nina agrees the sector call is good.

#### Sell Triggers

- if H2 order pickup fails to materialize
- if metal prices fall sharply
- if downstream mining demand weakens

> *"Och det kan vara en riktigt positiv historia för FLS. För de har inte full utnyttjningsgrad hos deras fabriker."*

---

### 🟠 MSFT — Microsoft | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Azure down ~20% YTD; question whether it delivers an 'intelligent cloud'; OpenAI IPO a potential catalyst. Added to Allstar portfolio.

#### Reasoning

Nina says Microsoft could be worth a look after a ~20% YTD drop, but the open question is whether it can keep pace in intelligent cloud; its murky OpenAI relationship and OpenAI's IPO could be positive or negative. In the Allstar portfolio Simon proposes adding Microsoft (and Infineon) while exiting Alphabet.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Azure/cloud growth keeps lagging AWS and Alphabet
- if OpenAI relationship turns negative
- if AI tool usage disappoints at next earnings

> *"Den har satt sig 20% år till dator eller i det leje. Men spörsmålet är, kan den komma med?"*

---

### 🟢 MRK — Merck | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Nina added; research pipeline repeatedly delivers (new Phase 3 in ulcerative colitis); AI seen aiding R&D/data work. Patent cliff 2028 a risk.

#### Reasoning

Nina has gone back into Merck because its research pipeline keeps producing, with a fresh Phase 3 asset (acquired biotech) in colon inflammation. She argues pharma R&D, especially Phase 1 data processing, benefits most from AI, enabling faster go/no-go decisions. Main concern is a 2028 patent expiry that must be defended.

#### Sell Triggers

- if 2028 patent expiries aren't offset by pipeline
- if Phase 3 colon program fails
- if R&D productivity falters

> *"Det är ju för att de igen och igen bevisar att deras research pipeline kan producera nya ting."*

---

### 🟢 HAS — Hasbro | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Magic: The Gathering trading cards; AI-resistant physical product with strong moat, high profitability; can't print enough to meet demand.

#### Reasoning

Victor added Hasbro as an AI-resistant physical-product business with a real moat: a 30-year-old game with ~1m loyal adult players who now have spending power, nostalgia-driven demand, scarcity/collectible value (cards sell for large sums), and pricing power. Supply constraint (can't print fast enough) was even a recent results headwind.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Magic player engagement declines
- if collectible/secondary-market demand cools
- if it over-prints and erodes scarcity value

> *"De kan faktiskt inte printa något kort till och i mödekom den efterspörseln där är."*

---

### 🟢 RTO — Rentokil | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Pest control; structurally growing, AI-resistant defensive business (global warming, urbanization). Lower upside than AI but steady.

#### Reasoning

Victor cites Rentokil as a comfortable 10-15 year hold: pest control is a secular growth area driven by global warming and urbanization, with low long-term uncertainty and resilience to AI disruption. The trade-off is lower potential return than booming AI names, but greater 'peace of mind'.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the structural pest-control growth story weakens
- if margins/pricing deteriorate

> *"Skadedjur är ett vuxent område. Det är global uppvarmning. Fler folk flyttar till byarna. Det skapar problem med råttor."*

---

### 🔴 SATS — EchoStar | **SELL** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 70%

Down 21% from cost; SpaceX proxy collapsed after SpaceX listing news; cancelled investor conference call signals weak governance. Being sold now.

#### Reasoning

EchoStar was held as a proxy for unlisted SpaceX/Starlink; once SpaceX moved toward listing, the proxy premium collapsed, compounded by cancelling its investor conference call (poor governance). Nina admits they forgot to sell in time; they exit now despite believing the long-term thesis (large SpaceX stake, incoming cash) is intact — but the Allstar portfolio is short-term.

#### Sell Triggers

- SpaceX listing removes the proxy rationale
- weak governance (cancelled investor call)
- short-term portfolio mandate, not long-term

> *"den är faktiskt nede med 21% från vårt köpskurs ... Så den må dessvärre ryge. Och nu säljer vi den."*

---

### 🟢 IFX — Infineon | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Added to the Allstar portfolio alongside Microsoft as semiconductor exposure.

#### Reasoning

When reshaping the Allstar portfolio (exiting EchoStar and Alphabet), Simon and Victor decide to add Microsoft and Infineon to keep meaningful tech/chip exposure rather than going fully out of tech. Limited specific thesis given beyond maintaining sector weighting.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the broad chip/AI theme de-rates
- if portfolio rebalancing shifts back to software

> *"Men jag tör sagtens ta Microsoft och Infineon."*

---

### 🟢 ASML — ASML | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

European AI supply-chain name with monopoly-type moat; held as a quality winner across the chip value chain.

#### Reasoning

Victor's AI strategy is to spread risk across the supply chain and own businesses with the strongest moats; in Europe that means ASML. The aim is to pick likely long-term AI winners protected from competition, accepting they'd still fall if the whole AI theme corrects.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the broad AI theme corrects
- if lithography demand/order momentum weakens

> *"Så i Europa så har du ASML, i Asien så har du TSMC och så Micron inför hukommelser."*

---

### 🟢 TSM — TSMC | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Asian foundry with strong moat; part of diversified AI supply-chain exposure among quality winners.

#### Reasoning

Like ASML, TSMC is held by Victor as a moaty, hard-to-displace leg of the AI supply chain. The thesis is to own quality leaders with high barriers to entry to maximize the chance of backing long-term AI winners, while acknowledging shared downside if AI sentiment turns.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the broad AI theme corrects
- if foundry demand or pricing weakens

> *"i Asien så har du TSMC"*

---

### 🟠 JNJ — Johnson & Johnson | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Preferred 'boring safe bet' among defensive consumer/healthcare names; favored over luxury names sensitive to Middle East geopolitics.

#### Reasoning

In the discussion of dull defensive ballast (Nike, Walmart, Adidas, L'Oreal), Nina prefers Johnson & Johnson, viewing luxury (L'Oreal) as less attractive given Middle East peace sensitivity. Simon sees such consumer staples as a fine portfolio component but too boring as a sole pick.

#### Sell Triggers

- if defensive consumer/healthcare loses relative appeal
- if pipeline/litigation risks rise

> *"Så jag vill mer i Johnson & Johnson."*

---

### 🟠 VWS — Vestas | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Up ~70% last year; panel regrets not owning it as the missing 'rocket'; flagged as a possible addition but also a risk.

#### Reasoning

Reviewing the lagging Allstar portfolio, Victor notes it lacks a rocket like Vestas, which surged ~70%. There is a half-suggestion it could be added if following the momentum logic, but it's also acknowledged as risky after such a run; no firm buy is made.

#### Sell Triggers

- if wind-sector momentum reverses after the strong run
- if it's added on momentum and the rally fades

> *"Vestas, som körde upp med 70 procent sist år."*

---
